zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood於1702年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州艾可時，她的父親Jacob Sherwood32歲，母親Elizabeth Radford30歲。她於1718年11月與John Maddox Sr在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州艾可結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1772年11月3日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州阿黑斯特阿黑斯特享年70歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州阿黑斯特阿黑斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood于1702年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州艾可时，她的父亲Jacob Sherwood32岁，母亲Elizabeth Radford30岁。她于1718年11月与John Maddox Sr在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州艾可结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1772年11月3日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州阿黑斯特阿黑斯特享年70岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州阿黑斯特阿黑斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Sherwood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of several places called with Old English scīr + wudu ‘bright or clear (perhaps sparsely-grown) wood’. In Yorkshire the name usually refers to Sherwood (Hall) in Eggborough (Yorkshire), but some examples could alternatively derive from Sherwood (House) in Stainforth (Yorkshire) or from the Nottinghamshire forest name in 2 below. The Devon name is from Sherwood (Green) in Yarnscombe (Devon). These are well evidenced as medieval placenames, and there may be similar but unrecorded places that could account for the medieval surname in other counties such as East Yorkshire, Norfolk, Oxfordshire, and Sussex. Sherwood occurs in minor placenames in Berkshire, Hampshire, Hertfordshire, Kent, North Yorkshire, Sussex, and Wiltshire, but they are recorded only in modern times and in many cases will have been named from the surname or perhaps in imitation of 2 below. This name was established in Ireland in the 17th century. See also Sherrard .
English: habitational name from Sherwood Forest in Nottinghamshire (‘the shire wood’).
Americanized form of some Jewish name.

variants.name: Sherrard
meanings: from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Shirard (Old English Scīrheard, composed of the elements scīr ‘bright’ and heard ‘hard, bold’). Alternatively, the name may be a nickname deriving from Middle English shir (Old English scīr) ‘bright, fair’, with the addition of the Anglo-Norman French suffix -ard.

local pronunciation of Sherwood , especially perhaps in Leicestershire, Kent, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Sussex, and Yorkshire. It may also have been much confused with Skerritt . This name, or the name in 1 above, has been established in Ireland (Derry) since the 17th century, along with Sherwood (in most provinces).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Woodson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire): habitational name for someone from Woodsome (a lost place in Batley), Woodsome in Farnley Tyas, or Wothersome, all in Yorkshire. The placenames derive from Old English wudu ‘wood’ + hūsum, dative plural of hūs ‘house’.
English (Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Ode, Odd(e) + son, with the addition of initial W-, a characteristic dialect feature in the south of Yorkshire.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood was born in 1702, in Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America, her father, Jacob Sherwood, was 32 and her mother, Elizabeth Radford, was 30. She married John Maddox Sr in November 1718, in Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 3 November 1772, in Amherst, Amherst, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 70, and was buried in Amherst, Amherst, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Woodson Sherwoodが1702年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州エセックスで生まれた時，父Jacob Sherwoodは32歳，母Elizabeth Radfordは30歳であった。1718年11月にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州エセックスでJohn Maddox Srと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1772年11月3日にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州アマーストアマーストで70歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州アマーストアマーストにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood(이)가 1702년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 에세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jacob Sherwood(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Radford(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1718년 11월 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 에세스에서 John Maddox Sr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1772년 11월 3일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 아헤르스트 아헤르스트에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 아헤르스트 아헤르스트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood nasceu em 1702, em Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America, seu pai, Jacob Sherwood, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Radford, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com John Maddox Sr em novembro de 1718, em Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 3 de novembro de 1772, em Amherst, Amherst, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Amherst, Amherst, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood родилась в 1702 г. в местности эссекс, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени Jacob Sherwood было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Radford, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Maddox Sr ноябрь 1718 г. в местности эссекс, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 3 ноября 1772 г. в местности амхирст, амхирст, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности амхирст, амхирст, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Woodson Sherwood nació en 1702, en Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America, su papá, Jacob Sherwood, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Radford, tenía 30 años. Se casó con John Maddox Sr en noviembre de 1718, en Essex, Virginia, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 3 de noviembre de 1772, en Amherst, Amherst, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Amherst, Amherst, Virginia, British Colonial America.
